title: 10 best herbal creams for bad breath

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree creams helps with bad breath because of its potent antibacterial properties. The cream contains oils that are known to target and eliminate the bacteria responsible for causing bad breath, such as Streptococcus mutans and Candida albicans. When applied topically, the tea tree oil in the cream works to reduce the levels of these bacteria on the tongue, gums, and mouth tissues, thereby helping to freshen and deodorize the breath. This natural approach can provide long-lasting relief from bad breath without harsh chemicals or artificial additives.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: The tea tree oil in these creams has potent antimicrobial properties that help kill bacteria and germs in the mouth, reducing bad breath caused by microbial overgrowth.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal tea tree creams may contain anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and reduce gum inflammation, which is a common cause of bad breath.
Antioxidant properties: Tea tree oil's antioxidant properties can help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to bad breath by reducing oxidative stress in the mouth.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their antimicrobial properties, tea tree leaves help combat bacteria that cause bad breath.
Roots: With their natural antibacterial and antifungal compounds, tea roots provide a strong foundation for fighting off oral infections.
remedy_recipe: Sterilize all equipment and workspace by submerging them in boiling water for 10 minutes.
Combine 100g of coconut oil, 50g of beeswax, and 20g of shea butter in a double boiler and heat until melted.
Add 5ml of melaleuca alternifolia essential oil to the mixture and stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass containers and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes or until cooled and solidified.
Once set, remove the creams from the refrigerator and use immediately or store in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with bad breath because of its natural antibacterial properties, which combat the growth of bacteria that cause halitosis. The menthol in peppermint also has a drying effect on the mouth, reducing the presence of volatile sulfur compounds (VSCs) that contribute to bad breath. Additionally, peppermint's cooling sensation stimulates saliva production, helping to wash away bacteria and food particles that can cause bad breath. By using herbal peppermint creams, individuals can effectively freshen their breath and enjoy a cleaner, more confident smile.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial agents: Peppermint creams contain antimicrobial compounds that help kill bacteria that cause bad breath by reducing their growth and eliminating them from the mouth and gums.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in peppermint cream can reduce swelling and irritation in the mouth and gums, which can contribute to bad breath, and also soothe gum inflammation caused by gum disease.

Refreshing and deodorizing: Peppermint's invigorating and cooling properties can stimulate saliva production, which helps neutralize acids and wash away bacteria that cause bad breath, while its deodorizing effects can leave the mouth feeling fresh and clean.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, peppermint roots are often used in herbal remedies to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that cause bad breath.
Barks: The barks of the peppermint plant contain a high concentration of menthol, which is a natural antibacterial agent that can help combat bad breath.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried peppermint leaves mentha x piperita of high quality in sufficient quantity for use.
Grind the dried peppermint leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle machine.
Mix 20 grams of the powdered peppermint leaves with 100 grams of petroleum jelly and 50 grams of coconut oil in a heatproof bowl.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes stirring occasionally until melted and well combined smooth consistency.
Pour the cooled and solidified cream into sterilized tubes or jars and store in cool dry place.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Purple coneflower's antimicrobial compounds, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, help kill bacteria in the mouth and throat that can cause bad breath.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in purple coneflower creams reduce swelling and redness in the gums and tongue, which can contribute to bad breath.
Antioxidant properties: Purple coneflower's antioxidant compounds neutralize free radicals that can damage oral tissues and lead to an overgrowth of bacteria that causes bad breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and antimicrobial compounds, which help combat bacteria that cause bad breath.
Buds: Contain flavonoids and terpenes that have antibacterial properties, effective against plaque-causing bacteria.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality echinacea purpurea flowers at dawn on a sunny day for optimal potency and freshness.
Dry the echinacea flowers in a low-temperature oven or dehydrator at 60°f for 24 hours to preserve their active compounds.
Grind 100 grams of dried echinacea flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 30 seconds.
Mix 2 tablespoons of beeswax pellets with 8 tablespoons of coconut oil and heat in a double boiler at 120°f for 10 minutes.
Combine the echinacea powder with the melted wax and oil mixture, stirring well to create a smooth and consistent cream texture within 15 minutes.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower creams helps with bad breath because of its unique blend of antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as Echinacea purpurea, work together to reduce plaque and gingivitis, eliminating the bacteria that cause foul-smelling odors in the mouth. Additionally, the cream's soothing properties help to calm irritated gums and mucous membranes, allowing for a fresher, healthier breath. By addressing the root causes of bad breath, purple coneflower creams provide a natural and effective solution for a cleaner, more confident smile.

Thyme creams helps with bad breath because of its potent antibacterial properties, which target the underlying causes of bad breath. The active compounds in thyme, such as thymol, possess natural deodorizing and anti-inflammatory effects that help to combat halitosis. When applied topically, thyme cream can reduce the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms in the mouth, tongue, and throat, leading to fresher breath and a cleaner mouth. Additionally, thyme's antimicrobial properties may also help to soothe and calm irritated gums and mucous membranes, further contributing to its effectiveness in combating bad breath.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Thyme's antimicrobial compounds, such as thymol, combat bacteria and other microorganisms responsible for causing bad breath (halitosis) by inhibiting their growth and killing them off.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can contribute to bad breath and discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: Thyme's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage oral tissues and contribute to bad breath, while also helping to protect against gum disease and other oral health issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots contain antibacterial compounds that help combat bacteria responsible for bad breath, reducing odor-causing sulfur compounds in the mouth.
Seeds: Thyme seeds possess anti-inflammatory properties that soothe gum inflammation and reduce redness, thereby alleviating bad breath symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and chop them finely using scissors.
Combine the chopped thymus vulgaris leaves with 10ml of cold-pressed olive oil in a glass jar.
Allow the mixture to infuse for 4 hours under room temperature without direct sunlight or heat.
Strain the infusion through cheesecloth into another glass container, discarding the solids and retaining 5ml of extract.
Mix the herbal extract with 10g of beeswax, 2g of coconut oil, and 1g of vitamin e oil in a double boiler until smooth consistency is achieved.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet marjoram creams helps with bad breath because of its natural antibacterial properties, which help to kill the bacteria that cause bad breath. The cream's active compounds, such as thymol and borneol, work to reduce the growth of these bacteria in the mouth, tongue, and throat, thereby eliminating the underlying causes of halitosis. Additionally, marjoram's soothing and anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce gum inflammation and irritation, further contributing to fresher breath.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Marjoram's antimicrobial compounds, such as thymol and borneol, help eliminate bacteria that cause bad breath by inhibiting their growth and reproduction.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Marjoram's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can contribute to bad breath by preventing the formation of biofilms that harbor odor-causing bacteria.

Antioxidant properties: Marjoram's antioxidant compounds, such as carnosol, neutralize free radicals that can damage oral tissues and contribute to bad breath by promoting gum disease and other oral health issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sweet marjoram roots are used in creams because they contain compounds that help reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that cause bad breath.
Leaves: The leaves of the sweet marjoram plant are used due to their antimicrobial properties, which help eliminate bad breath-causing germs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh origanum majorana leaves in early morning and dry them immediately under shade for preservation.
Combine 20 grams of dried origanum majorana leaves with 50 milliliters of coconut oil in a heat-resistant glass container for infusion.
Heat the mixture at low temperature of 60 degrees celsius for 30 minutes to extract the active compounds from the herb.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass container and discard the solids after cooling.
Mix the infused oil with 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler at low heat until fully melted and cooled to create a smooth cream consistency taking approximately 10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with bad breath because they contain antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that target the underlying causes of halitosis. The herb sage has been traditionally used to freshen breath and soothe gums, and when combined with other natural ingredients in a cream, it can effectively reduce bacteria and inflammation in the mouth. This leads to fresher breath, improved oral health, and a reduction in unpleasant odors.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial Properties: Sage's antimicrobial compounds, such as thujone and camphor, help combat bacteria and other microorganisms in the mouth that contribute to bad breath.
Antioxidant Properties: Sage's antioxidants, like rosmarinic acid, neutralize free radicals that can damage oral tissues and cause bad breath by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Sage's anti-inflammatory compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help reduce swelling and pain in the mouth and throat, which can contribute to bad breath and other oral health issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm gum inflammation, reducing bad breath.
Seeds: Sage seeds contain essential oils that combat bacteria and fungi causing bad breath, leaving the mouth feeling fresh and clean.
remedy_recipe: Harvest salvia officinalis leaves and dry them thoroughly using shade drying method.
Combine 20g of dried salvia officinalis leaves with 100ml of coconut oil in a double boiler setup.
Heat the mixture at low heat for 30 minutes to extract the bioactive compounds from the herbs.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and discard the solids, leaving you with 50ml of herbal oil infusion.
Mix the herbal oil infusion with 100g of beeswax and 20ml of jojoba oil, then whip until a smooth and creamy texture is achieved.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass creams helps with bad breath because of its natural antibacterial properties, which combat the underlying causes of halitosis. The cream's active ingredients, such as citral and geraniol, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that contribute to bad breath. Additionally, lemongrass has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the gums, reducing inflammation and gum disease, a common precursor to bad breath. By targeting these underlying issues, lemongrass creams provide a holistic solution for freshening and revitalizing the mouth.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Lemongrass creams contain compounds like citral and geraniol, which exhibit antimicrobial activity against bacteria that cause bad breath, reducing their growth and eliminating odors.

Deodorizing properties: The aromatic oils in lemongrass, such as limonene and beta-caryophyllene, have natural deodorizing properties that help neutralize volatile sulfur compounds (VSCs) responsible for bad breath, leaving a fresh and clean scent.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemongrass creams may reduce inflammation in the mouth and gums, which can contribute to bad breath by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and reducing redness and swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemongrass roots contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation and kill bacteria in the mouth, effectively treating bad breath.
Rhyzomes: The rhizome of lemongrass is used as it has a high concentration of citral, a natural antibacterial compound that helps eliminate germs causing bad breath.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves and clean them thoroughly under running water for at least 10 minutes.
Dry the cleaned cymbopogon citratus leaves in a warm oven or dehydrator at 60°c for 6 hours.
Grind the dried cymbopogon citratus leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or food processor with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil for 5 minutes.
Mix the ground herb powder with 100ml of distilled water and 10% glycerin to create a smooth paste, let it sit at room temperature for 24 hours.
Pour the paste into sterilized containers and refrigerate at 5°c for up to 6 weeks or freeze for longer storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary creams helps with bad breath because of its potent antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb's essential oils, such as camphor and bornyl acetate, have been shown to reduce the growth of harmful bacteria in the mouth, including those that cause bad breath. Additionally, rosemary's natural astringent properties help to dry out the mucous membranes in the mouth, reducing the likelihood of odor-causing volatile sulfur compounds from forming. This results in fresher, cleaner breath and a reduction in halitosis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Rosemary's antimicrobial compounds help combat bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can cause bad breath by reducing their growth and eliminating them from the mouth and gums.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and irritation in the mouth and gums, which can contribute to bad breath by minimizing inflammation and promoting a healthy oral environment.
Antioxidant properties: Rosemary's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage oral tissues and contribute to bad breath by protecting the mouth and gums from oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to combat bad breath due to their antimicrobial properties, which help eliminate bacteria and other microorganisms responsible for halitosis.

Buds: Rosemary buds are often used in traditional remedies for bad breath because they contain high concentrations of antioxidants and essential oils that can help reduce inflammation and freshen the breath.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosemary leaves and wash them thoroughly with distilled water to remove any dirt or debris.
Dry the rosemary leaves in a clean, dry environment for at least 24 hours using paper towels.
Grind dried rosemary leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle then sift through a strainer.
Mix 20 grams of ground rosemary powder with 100 milliliters of coconut oil and 50 milliliters of beeswax in a heat-resistant bowl.
Heat the mixture for 10 minutes at a temperature of 60 degrees celsius using a double boiler then let it cool to room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with bad breath because they contain natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory compounds that combat the bacteria responsible for halitosis. The active ingredients in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to reduce the growth of anaerobic bacteria that thrive in the mouth and contribute to bad breath. Additionally, the warming properties of ginger help to stimulate saliva production, which helps to neutralize acids and remove volatile sulfur compounds that cause bad breath.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can contribute to bad breath by reducing the production of saliva and causing gum irritation.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial compounds found in ginger, including gingerol and sesquiterpenes, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause bad breath, such as Streptococcus mutans and Fusobacterium nucleatum.

Digestive stimulant properties: Ginger's ability to stimulate digestion and relieve nausea can help reduce halitosis by reducing the presence of stomach acid in the mouth and throat, which can contribute to bad breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antimicrobial compounds, ginger roots are effective at reducing bacteria that cause bad breath.
Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stem of the ginger plant, rhyzomes contain volatile oils that help to kill germs and freshen breath.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger root of zingiber officinale and wash it thoroughly under running water.
Dry the ginger root in a warm oven at 150°f for 2 hours or air dry it for 24 hours.
Grind 20 grams of dried ginger root into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 10 grams of the powdered ginger with 50 milliliters of coconut oil and 30 millilitres of beeswax in a heat-resistant container.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture reaches 180°f.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ironwort creams helps with bad breath because of their unique properties that combat bacteria and reduce inflammation in the mouth. The herbs used in these creams have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory compounds that work to eliminate the sources of bad breath, such as volatile sulfur compounds produced by oral bacteria. By reducing the growth of these bacteria and soothing inflamed tissues, herbal ironwort creams help to freshen breath and leave a pleasant, healthy mouthfeel.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Ironwort creams contain antimicrobial compounds that help kill bacteria and other microorganisms that can cause bad breath, reducing their ability to produce volatile sulfur compounds (VSCs) that contribute to halitosis.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Ironwort creams have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can contribute to bad breath by creating an environment conducive to bacterial growth.

Antioxidant activity: Ironwort creams contain antioxidants that can help neutralize free radicals that can damage oral tissues and create a conducive environment for bacteria to grow, leading to bad breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help to reduce inflammation in the mouth and eliminate bad breath causing bacteria.
Barks: The barks contain tannins, which have antiseptic and antibacterial properties, making them effective against oral infections that cause bad breath.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried sideritis scardica leaves and stems from a reputable source.
Combine the harvested material with 10 grams of beeswax in a double boiler and melt at 180 degrees fahrenheit for 30 minutes.
Add 40 milliliters of sweet almond oil to the melted wax mixture and stir well to combine.
Continue heating the mixture at 160 degrees fahrenheit for an additional 20 minutes until it reaches a consistency suitable for pouring into containers.
Allow the cream to cool and set completely before transferring it to sterilized jars with tight-fitting lids, storing in a dark place for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with bad breath because its essential oils, particularly linalool and linalyl acetate, have natural antibacterial and antifungal properties. These compounds help to reduce the growth of bacteria that can cause halitosis (bad breath), as well as eliminate fungal infections in the mouth. Additionally, English lavender's soothing and calming effects can also alleviate gum inflammation and irritation, further contributing to fresher breath and a healthier oral environment.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Lavender's antimicrobial activity helps to reduce the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that cause bad breath, such as streptococcus, staphylococcus, and E. coli.

Anti-inflammatory property: English lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds, like linalool, can help to reduce inflammation in the mouth, gums, and tongue, which is often a contributing factor to bad breath.
Antioxidant property: Lavender's antioxidants can help to neutralize free radicals that can damage oral tissues and contribute to bad breath, while also promoting overall oral health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the English lavender plant contain a high concentration of volatile oils, which have antibacterial properties that help combat bad breath-causing bacteria.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavender flowers from lavandula angustifolia plants for optimal potency.
Dry the lavender flowers using a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours.
Combine 20% dried lavender flowers with 80% sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days to allow for infusion and extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth and add beeswax at a ratio of 10:1 to create a smooth cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with bad breath because they contain compounds that have natural antibacterial properties, which help to eliminate bacteria in the mouth responsible for causing halitosis. The anethole present in fennel also reduces inflammation and irritation in the gums and tongue, which can contribute to bad breath. Additionally, fennel's digestive benefits can help alleviate gum disease and other oral health issues that often lead to bad breath. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and dry them in the shade for 24 hours.
Grind dried foeniculum vulgare leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 20 grams of powdered foeniculum vulgare with 10 grams of coconut oil and 5 grams of beeswax.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax melts.
Pour the cooled cream into sterilized glass jars and store them in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Coriander creams helps with bad breath because of its natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh coriandrum sativum leaves and clean them thoroughly with distilled water to remove dirt and debris.
Dry the cleaned coriandrum sativum leaves in a warm oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 6 hours to reduce moisture content.
Combine one tablespoon of dried coriandrum sativum powder with two tablespoons of coconut oil and three drops of peppermint essential oil in a small bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the coriandrum sativum powder is fully incorporated into the coconut oil.
Transfer the cream to a sterilized glass jar, label it clearly, and store it in the refrigerator at a consistent temperature of 40 degrees fahrenheit for up to 14 days.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality calendula officinalis flowers and clean them thoroughly in cold water.
Infuse one cup of dried calendula flowers in two cups of boiling water for ten minutes.
Combine the infused liquid with eight ounces of cold-pressed olive oil and heat it gently for thirty minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container and discard solids.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken completely at room temperature over six hours.

remedy_recipe: Harvest artemisia absinthium leaves in abundance for a minimum of 250 grams.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm dark place for 7-10 days or until crispy.
Combine 150 grams of dried artemisia absinthium with 50 grams of coconut oil and 20 grams of beeswax.
Melt the mixture over low heat for 15-20 minutes stirring occasionally to combine ingredients.
Strain the cooled mixture into a glass jar using cheesecloth or a coffee filter for 2 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with bad breath because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The gel-like substance found in aloe vera has been shown to reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can help alleviate symptoms of bad breath. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera gel from 3-4 leaves of organic aloe barbadensis weighing approximately 200 grams.
Combine 150 milliliters of distilled water with 15 grams of dried peppermint leaves and simmer in a double boiler for 20 minutes.
Strain the peppermint mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bowl removing excess liquid and solids.
Mix 100 grams of aloe vera gel with 30 milliliters of coconut oil and 5 milliliters of vitamin e oil in a stainless steel mixing bowl.
Pour the combined herbal cream into sterilized glass jars and store them in the refrigerator at 40 degrees fahrenheit for up to 6 weeks.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried helichrysum italicum flowers and store them in an airtight container for later use.
Combine the harvested helichrysum italicum flowers with 100ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep at room temperature for 4 weeks shaking the jar daily to infuse the oils with the herb's properties.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container and discard the solids.
Mix 20% of the infused oil with beeswax and vitamin e oil in a double boiler until fully melted and cooled to create a smooth creamy consistency.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Myrtle's antimicrobial compounds help combat the growth of bacteria and fungi that cause bad breath, reducing the production of volatile sulfur compounds (VSCs) responsible for halitosis.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Myrtle's anti-inflammatory compounds may reduce inflammation in the oral cavity, which can contribute to bad breath by irritating gum tissue and disrupting the natural balance of oral flora.

Antioxidant properties: Myrtle's antioxidant compounds help neutralize free radicals that can damage oral tissues and disrupt the mouth's natural defense mechanisms, potentially contributing to bad breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the myrtle plant are valued for their antimicrobial properties, which help combat bacteria responsible for bad breath.
Barks: Myrtle bark is rich in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in reducing inflammation in the mouth and eliminating bad breath.
Seeds: Myrtle seeds have been used in traditional medicine for their astringent and antibacterial properties, which help to reduce bacteria that cause bad breath.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry fresh myrtus communis leaves in a warm place for at least two weeks or until crispy.
Grind dried myrtus communis leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine 20 grams of ground myrtus communis powder with 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil and 50 milliliters of coconut oil in a heat-proof glass bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the oils have fully infused the herb extract.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass jar and let cool to room temperature before storing.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for bad breath are natural remedies made from a combination of herbs, essential oils, and other plant-based ingredients designed to combat halitosis. These creams work by reducing bacteria growth in the mouth, neutralizing volatile sulfur compounds that cause bad breath, and freshening the breath with their antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. Examples of herbal creams that help with bad breath include Peppermint oil cream, Tea Tree oil cream, and Sage oil cream. By using these creams, individuals can say goodbye to embarrassing social situations and hello to confidence-boosting fresh breath.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat bad breath with herbal creams is to identify the underlying cause of halitosis. Herbal creams containing ingredients like peppermint, sage, and tea tree oil have natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce plaque, prevent gum disease, and eliminate odor-causing bacteria. Apply the cream topically to the tongue, gums, and cheeks, and also use a toothbrush to gently scrub your tongue and the roof of your mouth.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to bad breath that are treated with herbal creams are acne, eczema, and psoriasis. These conditions can cause skin lesions, inflammation, and irritation, leading to discomfort and embarrassment. Herbal creams containing ingredients like tea tree oil, chamomile, and aloe vera have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which help soothe and heal affected areas, reducing redness, itching, and scaling.
